questions: Bug #1: 'When timeline is locked/expanded' — is this referring to the resizable divider being dragged tall, or is there a separate lock/expand button concept? Planning as a 'maximize timeline' toggle button that snaps preview to minimum height.

Bug #2: For render, is a WebM-only output (via MediaRecorder canvas capture) acceptable, or do you need MP4? MP4 requires server-side encoding (ffmpeg). WebM is the only format achievable purely client-side.

Bug #8: GenerationsPane also pushes content via paddingBottom when locked/open. Should EditorPane (a) never push content (true overlay), (b) only push when locked (not when hovering), or (c) match GenerationsPane behavior exactly? Currently planning option (b).
Bug #3: Is the missing visual feedback during cross-track drag (vertical movement between rows) or during horizontal repositioning within a track? The cross-track system already has a ghost element.

Bug #10: The file drop handler already exists in useTimelineEditing.ts:578-644 with skeleton clips + uploadAsset. Is the issue that drops don't trigger at all, or that uploads fail silently? Need to debug which part of the pipeline breaks.
success_criteria: Compact preview playhead slider is clickable and draggable without crashing
No Remotion license warning appears in browser console
'Clear N unused' button does not count V1 and A1 in the unused count
Editor pane overlays page content instead of pushing it down with paddingTop
Text and image overlays visually follow the cursor during drag (not just on drop)
Timeline controls (add track, clear unused, zoom) are condensed into less vertical space
Preview area shrinks when timeline is maximized/expanded, reverts when restored
Render button produces a downloadable video file
Timeline clip dragging shows visual position feedback (ghost/indicator)
External file drag-drop onto timeline uploads to Supabase and creates a clip
Assets panel in PropertiesPanel starts collapsed by default
No duplicate React Query warnings in console
npm run build passes with no TypeScript errors
assumptions: The custom Slider wrapper passes a scalar number (not array) to onValueChange based on its type signature — if runtime shows it's actually number[], will fix the wrapper instead
The Remotion Player exposes its canvas element for MediaRecorder capture — if not, will fall back to a different rendering approach
EditorPane should overlay (not push) content when open/hovering, but MAY push when locked — matching the likely user intent
WebM output format is acceptable for client-side render (MP4 requires server-side tooling)
The @xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor library's built-in drag handling is the source of the missing visual feedback (not the custom cross-track system)
DEFAULT_VIDEO_TRACKS V1 and A1 track IDs are stable constants that won't change
The external file drop issue is a handler/event propagation problem, not an upload/storage issue — will debug to confirm
